---
title: "Build with us"
description: "Add a component, extend a workflow, or improve the docs."
order: 0
listing:
  id: contributing
  type: table
  contents:
    - getting-started.qmd
    - project-structure.qmd
    - creating-components.qmd
    - creating-pipelines.qmd
    - running-tests.qmd
  fields: [title, description]
  field-display-names:
    title: "Step"
    description: "What it covers"
  sort: false
  filter-ui: false
  sort-ui: false
---

OpenPipeline is open source. The path in, in order:

:::{#contributing}
:::

The pull-request, build, and release flow — how a merge to `main` triggers a CI
build and how a release publishes documentation automatically — is covered under
[Project structure → Versioning and branching](project-structure.qmd#sec-versioning).

Stuck on something specific? The [FAQ](faq.qmd) covers common component-development
questions (external resources, `__merge__`, inspecting the generated Dockerfile,
reporting bugs).
